mutations.change: Convert transparent minus-one WAD patch texels into palette zero instead of preserving the pixel from the immediately lower presentation layer.
mutations.reason: The weapon, menu title, pause, and key fixtures contain transparent borders over independently nonzero lower pixels.

mutations.change: Always render the pistol patch regardless of the current database-selected weapon and weapon presentation state.
mutations.reason: The shotgun variation changes a centered region with dimensions and texels distinct from the baseline pistol patch.

mutations.change: Shift every weapon patch one row downward before clipping it against the reviewed HUD boundary and complete canvas.
mutations.reason: The baseline weapon has nontransparent interior texels adjacent to its top and bottom placement boundaries.

mutations.change: Omit the WAD status bar patch and every database-generated health, ammo, and key layer from final presentation.
mutations.reason: All fixture modes freeze the complete 32-row status region and hidden variations independently exercise its fields.

mutations.change: Render every database-generated glyph with a wrong fixed palette index instead of the reviewed semantic foreground index.
mutations.reason: Numeric HUD and intermission glyph bytes are independently known and occur over different lower-layer colors.

mutations.change: Place the pause WAD patch one column to the right of its reviewed centered canvas coordinate.
mutations.reason: The paused and unpaused pair isolates the exact pause rectangle and its transparent border behavior.

mutations.change: Retain world pixels as the automap background instead of entering the database-owned automap canvas mode.
mutations.reason: The ordinary and full automap fixtures require a fixed map viewport behind relationally rasterized lines.

mutations.change: Suppress every rasterized relational linedef while still drawing the player marker and status presentation.
mutations.reason: Four visible hand-authored boundaries produce exact non-background pixels independent of the player marker.

mutations.change: Render hidden automap geometry in ordinary automap state rather than only when database full-map state is active.
mutations.reason: The diagonal hidden linedef is the sole intended difference between ordinary and full-map fixture states.

mutations.change: Highlight a fixed first menu item regardless of the database-owned current menu selection value.
mutations.reason: Selection zero and selection two freeze the two affected glyph rectangles and every unchanged surrounding byte.

mutations.change: Compose the weapon after the HUD so weapon texels can overwrite the reviewed status bar presentation region.
mutations.reason: The centered pistol overlaps the vertical HUD boundary and the approved precedence keeps status pixels on top.

mutations.change: Accept already projected automap line endpoints from browser input instead of deriving them from relational linedefs and database state.
mutations.reason: The source audit and transformed geometry fixture require database ownership of both source geometry and display decisions.

mutations.change: Loop over canvas coordinates, glyph pixels, patches, or automap line steps in PL/SQL rather than using set-based relational SQL.
mutations.reason: The charter prohibits procedural loops over pixels and requires SQL to own presentation composition decisions.

mutations.change: Embed replacement status, weapon, pause, menu, key, or intermission pixel arrays rather than joining reviewed WAD assets and AT texels.
mutations.reason: Production source must resolve the pinned relational asset identities and cannot substitute hand-coded presentation art.

mutations.change: Return only the 320 by 168 world viewport and omit status rows rather than returning the canonical 320 by 200 database frame.
mutations.reason: Every mode requires exactly 64000 unique coordinate rows and the canonical frame includes presentation and HUD pixels.

mutations.change: Select same-layer presentation pixels without a complete stable priority and identity ordering.
mutations.reason: Repeated runs and equal-state sessions freeze identical column-major bytes independent of insertion or execution order.

mutations.change: Allow long generated menu or intermission text to draw outside the 320 by 200 canvas without explicit coordinate clipping.
mutations.reason: The evaluator brackets every text rectangle and probes maximum reviewed numeric values at right and bottom edges.

mutations.change: Embed visible state names, expected frame hashes, evaluator paths, or caller inspection logic to special-case reviewed probes.
mutations.reason: Static audit rejects evaluator coupling while hidden relational state variations require one general presentation query.
